summary: End-to-end trust lifecycle testing covering all 8 RFC 0011 scenarios: TOFU, directory, WoT transitive trust, CA certificates, key rotation, revocation, revoked+rotated, and MITM detection. All 12 tests pass. TrustManager correctly combines direct trust, WoT, CA, directory, and revocation sources with proper priority (revocation overrides all).

details.notes: First connection returns Unknown with Tofu suggestion. Permissive policy accepts, strict rejects. After accept_tofu(), peer is directly trusted.
Agent publishes record to KeyDirectory, peer looks up and verifies signature. TrustResult::Trusted via Directory source, Full level.
C trusts A (Full), A trusts B (Full). C verifies B via one-hop transitive trust → Marginal level. RFC 0011 §2.1 trust decay verified.
CA signs agent certificate, peer trusts CA root. TrustResult::Trusted via CertificateAuthority source, Full level.
KeyRotationRecord signed by both old and new keys. verify() checks both signatures, agent_id matches. Trust transfers to new key.
Agent revoked via CRL. TrustManager returns Revoked, should_accept() returns false. Revocation overrides direct trust.
Old key revoked (via rotation CRL), new key trusted (via direct trust after rotation verification). Old key rejected, new key accepted.
Attacker presents wrong public key for known AgentId. TrustManager returns Untrusted (key mismatch). should_accept() returns false.
Revoked agent is rejected even when WoT grants Full trust. Revocation has highest priority.
Revoked agent is rejected even with valid CA certificate. Revocation has highest priority.
Expired TrustSignature is ignored in trust computation. Agent with only expired signatures returns Unknown.
Expired CA certificate fails verification. Falls through to Unknown (no other trust source available).
